title: rhododendron oxycoccoides
intro: Rhododendron oxycoccoides, also known as the pink or lowbush azalea, is a low-growing, woody shrub native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. Rhododendron oxycoccoides is also valued for its potential health benefits, including its ability to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its uses should be guided by qualified healthcare professionals.
